retrieved_docs.title: New Mexico Medical Practice Act Review and Understanding -- Issues of Concern -- Section 15. Section 61-6-15 NMSA 1978 (being Laws 1969, Chapter 46, Section 6, as amended) is amended to read:
retrieved_docs.content: C. If evidence fails to establish to the board's satisfaction that the licensee is competent and of good moral character or if evidence shows that the licensee has not complied with the terms of probation, the board may revoke or suspend the license. If a license to practice in this state is suspended, the license holder may not practice during the term of suspension. A person whose license has been revoked or suspended by the board and who after that practices or attempts or offers to practice in New Mexico, unless the period of suspension has expired or been modified by the board or the license reinstated, is guilty of a felony and shall be punished as provided in Section 61-6-20 NMSA 1978. D. "Unprofessional or dishonorable conduct," as used in this section, means, but is not limited to because of enumeration, the conduct of a licensee that includes the following: (1) procuring, aiding, or abetting a criminal abortion; (2) employing a person to solicit patients for the licensee;

retrieved_docs.title: Emancipated Minor -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: In general, adolescents can consent to specific medical services that provide an important public health benefit without parental permission or notification. [7] Some of these services are federally funded and consistently available in most states, including the preventative, contraception, and reproductive health services provided by the Title X Family Planning Program of the Public Health Service Act. [2] The US Supreme Court has upheld the constitutional right of minors to choose abortion but has also upheld the state’s right to impose requirements for parental involvement as long as “adequate provision for judicial bypass” is available for minors where parental involvement is not in their best interest. retrieved_docs.title: Florida Nurse Practice Act: Laws and Rules for Florida Nurses -- Issues of Concern -- Penalty for Theft or Reproduction of an Examination (Section 456.018)
retrieved_docs.content: In addition to or in lieu of any disciplinary action under section 456.072, the theft of an examination, whether in whole or in part, or the act of reproducing or copying any examination administered by the department—regardless of the extent or method—constitutes a third-degree felony. This offense is punishable as provided in sections 775.082, 775.083, or 775.084.[ 2024 Florida Statutes ]

retrieved_docs.title: Patient Confidentiality -- Other Issues -- Civil and Criminal Violations
retrieved_docs.content: When the healthcare institution fails to comply satisfactorily, OCR may impose civil monetary penalties based on the seriousness of the non-compliance. The amount of monetary fine is usually up to the discretion of the secretary of HHS. It depends on the extent and nature of the harm due to the violation. In almost all cases, the secretary is not permitted to impose any civil penalty for a violation that is corrected within 4 to 6 weeks. All criminal violations of HIPAA are handled by the Department of Justice, who, in addition to civil penalties, may add other fines depending on the severity of the violation.

Health providers are seen as helpers of God’s will. Most surgical procedures are permitted. No restrictions on amputations, biopsies, blood or blood products, medications, amputations

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Practice Issues in American Indians and Alaska Natives -- Etiology -- Mistrust in the Federal System
retrieved_docs.content: The social injustice continued even after the establishment of IHS. Multiple instances of social injustices (like forced sterilization, unethical research, and clinical misconduct in the name of research) have come forth that explain the mistrust of the AIAN community in the federal health services. [8] The most recent of these is the sterilization of American Indian women between 1973-76 by either force or coercion to consent. Several high-level lawsuits like Havasupai Tribe v Arizona State University Board of Regents and Tilousi v Arizona State University Board of Regents mark misrepresentation of research projects in the AIAN community. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Forensic Autopsy -- Issues of Concern -- Aims and Objectives of a Forensic Autopsy
retrieved_docs.content: However, it is noteworthy that the final verdict regarding the manner of death is decided by the Court of Law in most jurisdictions worldwide since opinion on the manner of death is based not just on medical evidence but more so on other circumstantial pieces of evidence. Medical evidence is only one piece of the puzzle. In the case of a fetal autopsy or an autopsy of a neonate, the following are the specific medicolegal objectives: To determine the intra-uterine/gestational age of the fetus, ultimately aiming to know whether the fetus was viable or not (to assess the viability of the fetus) If viable, to determine whether it was born alive (live birth) or dead (dead birth/stillbirth) If born alive, to determine the period of survival after birth, the cause, and manner of death

retrieved_docs.title: Diversity Goals for Evaluation and Treatment of American Indians and Alaska Natives -- Issues of Concern -- Historical & Sociopolitical Context
retrieved_docs.content: Forced assimilation practices were prevalent throughout history as well. In 1883, the “Code of Indian Offences” was established to outlaw many traditional AIAN practices, and these policies remained in effect until 1934. During this time, AIAN people who continued to practice their traditions risked being incarcerated. [5] Around the same time, the Bureau of Indian Affairs started forcibly removing children from their homes and placing them in federally operated boarding schools, where they were punished for speaking their language. [16] [17] Some of these children were removed from their families for many years, some never returned home, and some died from disease and homesickness at the boarding schools. [16]

Clinical Significance No clinical issues that inhibit healthcare.

retrieved_docs.title: Death Certification -- Function
retrieved_docs.content: The pronouncement is a terminology that denotes the time when an individual is found to be legally dead. In forensic or medical examiner (ME) cases, this might not always correspond to the actual time of death. An individual living alone or in a remote area might have been dead for days or rarely years before the pronouncement is made.

retrieved_docs.title: Expert Witness -- Introduction -- Standard of Care
retrieved_docs.content: The standard for admission of expert testimony was set in 1923 in Frye v. United States, 293 F. 1013 (D.C. Cir. 1923). The holding of this case was quite simple. It stated that an expert opinion is admissible if the scientific technique on which the opinion is based is “generally accepted” as reliable in the relevant scientific community. This case involved polygraph testing (1923) when lie detector evidence was not widely accepted. It was, however, the opinion of the court that there was sufficient support in the scientific community to admit the evidence as from an established source. This is called The Frye Standard . Over the following decades, Frye became the standard for acceptance of evidence. It was first used in criminal cases, but in the 1980s, it was utilized in toxic tort cases. After decades of use, critics complained that it could no longer be reliably used as scientific principles became more complex. Those complaints, misapprehensions, and criticisms led to Daubert .

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2391
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion, also described as termination of pregnancy, is classified under the term الإجهاض المحرض, which denotes an induced abortion.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 3 -- Community 2500
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion (termination of pregnancy) is classified as a specific instance of إنهاء الحمل (جراحي), indicating that it falls under the broader category of surgical termination of pregnancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 4 -- Community 2790
retrieved_docs.content: إنهاء الحمل - اختياري, which denotes an elective termination of pregnancy, is clinically associated with تهديد بالإجهاض, a condition described as threatened abortion. This clinical association indicates that the two obstetric entities share overlapping concerns, and that the presence of a threatened abortion may influence considerations regarding elective pregnancy termination in patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 1798
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy complications (مضاعفات الحمل) encompass a range of conditions, including fetal disorder (اضطراب جنيني), threatened abortion (تهديد بالإجهاض), and gestational diabetes (داء السكري أثناء الحمل). Both threatened abortion and gestational diabetes are recognized as specific types of pregnancy complications. Clinically, optional termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) is associated with the management of gestational diabetes and also with threatened abortion, indicating that termination may be considered as a therapeutic option in these particular obstetric scenarios.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2007
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy observable is employed as an interpretive tool for إجهاض معتاد, indicating that observable signs and findings during pregnancy can be used to recognize or assess the condition of recurrent miscarriage. This relationship underscores the clinical importance of monitoring pregnancy‑related observations to identify and manage habitual abortion effectively.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2815
retrieved_docs.content: In epidemiology, the term “Allowed qualifier” is applied as a classification descriptor for the condition إجهاض تلقائي غير كامل (incomplete spontaneous abortion), indicating that this qualifier is permitted when recording or analyzing cases of this type of miscarriage.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2138
retrieved_docs.content: Surgical termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل (جراحي)) is classified as a member of the broader clinical grouping “termination of pregnancy and risk of miscarriage” (إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (SMQ)). This relationship indicates that the specific procedure of surgically ending a pregnancy is encompassed within the larger safety‑monitoring and terminology framework that addresses both pregnancy termination methods and the associated risk of miscarriage, facilitating comprehensive categorization and assessment in medical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 4 -- Community 2811
retrieved_docs.content: Other complications of pregnancy are clinically associated with إنهاء الحمل – اختياري (optional termination of pregnancy) and also have a relationship with اضطراب رحمي (uterine disorder). This indicates that uterine disorders are considered among the other complications of pregnancy and that optional termination of pregnancy can be clinically linked to these pregnancy‑related complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 3 -- Community 2060
retrieved_docs.content: Delivery finding is a clinical observation that can be classified as a type (isa) of “baby is coming out,” indicating a live birth, and also as a type (isa) of “ولادة جنين ميت” (dead‑fetus delivery). The condition “إجهاض فائت” (missed abortion) has a relationship with “ولادة جنين ميت,” reflecting that a missed abortion may be associated with or result in a dead‑fetus delivery. This integrated view highlights the spectrum of delivery outcomes—from live birth to non‑viable fetal delivery—and underscores the importance of accurately distinguishing these scenarios for appropriate patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 1 -- Community 149
retrieved_docs.content: إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) is associated with ولادة جنين ميت (birth of a dead fetus). The group إنهاء الحمل (جراحي) includes the safety‑monitoring query مجموعة إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (SMQ), which itself is a member of the broader ولادة جنين ميت category. Collectively, these relationships delineate a comprehensive taxonomy in which Obstetrics excision functions as the central surgical category for managing abortion, miscarriage, ectopic pregnancy, and related obstetric interventions, with numerous specific techniques, Arabic terminology, and safety‑monitoring concepts organized as subordinate elements.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 5 -- Community 2915
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Allowed qualifier” is recognized as an etiological descriptor that can be applied to the conditions premature birth (ولادة قبل الأوان), uterine scar (قَرْحَة مَعِدِيّة), and threatened abortion (تهديد بالإجهاض).

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 2 -- Community 926
retrieved_docs.content: إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) is linked to ولادة جنين ميت (birth of a dead fetus), indicating that missed abortions may be associated with stillbirth outcomes. The clinical observation category Pregnancy observable is used to interpret إجهاض معتاد (recurrent miscarriage), highlighting its role in recognizing repeated pregnancy loss. إجهاض معتاد is classified as a member of the standardized medical query إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (SMQ) (Termination of pregnancy and risk of miscarriage), which aggregates conditions related to pregnancy termination and miscarriage risk. Within this SMQ, إنهاء الحمل (جراحي) (surgical termination of pregnancy) is included as a specific type of termination. The SMQ itself is associated with ولادة جنين ميت, reflecting that the collection of termination and miscarriage‑risk concepts is relevant to stillbirth scenarios. Together, these relationships illustrate how إجهاض فائت, إجهاض معتاد, surgical termination, and ولادة جنين ميت are interconnected within the broader framework of pregnancy loss and termination risk assessment, aiding clinicians in systematic identification and management of adverse pregnancy outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 3 -- Community 2407
retrieved_docs.content: إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) is linked to ولادة جنين ميت (birth of a dead fetus), indicating that a missed abortion can be associated with the delivery of a non‑viable fetus. Moreover, ولادة جنين ميت is a member of the Standardized MedDRA Query (SMQ) titled إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (Termination of pregnancy and risk of abortion), which groups together adverse events related to pregnancy termination and fetal loss. These connections illustrate how missed abortion and stillbirth are interrelated concepts within broader safety‑monitoring frameworks for adverse pregnancy outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 4 -- Community 2853
retrieved_docs.content: Elective termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) is clinically associated with both forceps delivery (الولادة بالملقط) and threatened abortion (تهديد بالإجهاض), indicating that patients who undergo an elective termination may be at increased risk for experiencing a threatened miscarriage and may later require assisted vaginal delivery with forceps. Recognizing these associations can help clinicians monitor for early signs of abortion and plan appropriate delivery strategies in subsequent pregnancies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 4 -- Community 2900
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Allowed qualifier” is employed as an enzymological descriptor for the condition إجهاض تلقائي غير كامل (incomplete spontaneous abortion), indicating that enzymatic factors are recognized as permissible qualifiers when characterizing this type of abortion and underscoring the relevance of enzyme studies in understanding its pathophysiology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 2 -- Community 798
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion (termination of pregnancy) is a broad medical concept that classifies الإجهاض المحرض (induced abortion) and is positioned as a child of إنهاء الحمل (جراحي) (surgical termination of pregnancy). The surgical termination category encompasses the standardized query إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (SMQ) (termination of pregnancy and risk of abortion), and this SMQ is itself a member of ولادة جنين ميت (stillbirth of a dead fetus). In addition, إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) is described as having a relationship with ولادة جنين ميت, linking this type of incomplete pregnancy loss to the outcome of stillbirth. Together, these hierarchical and associative links illustrate how various forms of pregnancy termination and related adverse outcomes are organized within medical classification systems, aiding clinicians and coders in accurately identifying and managing these conditions.
